core_mechanism: a BBP-type spectral phase transition of a low-rank-perturbed random covariance matrix, in which the top (extreme) eigenvalue detaches from the bulk of the noise spectrum only once the signal strength exceeds a sharp critical threshold set by the interplay of perturbation parameters
brief_reason: The abstract proves that the extreme eigenvalues of the SMM covariance matrix exhibit a phase transition in high-dimensional regimes governing when embedded signals (spikes) become detectable, giving sharp threshold bounds on signal strength, correlation, and mixture weights.
